children.heading: Qualified mortgage bond defined
children.label: 1 Qualified mortgage bond defined
children.text: For purposes of this title, the term “qualified mortgage bond” means a bond which is issued as part of a qualified mortgage issue.

children.label: i
children.text: all proceeds of such issue (exclusive of issuance costs and a reasonably required reserve) are to be used to finance owner-occupied residences,

children.label: ii
children.text: such issue meets the requirements of subsections (c), (d), (e), (f), (g), (h), (i), and (m)(7),

children.label: iii
children.text: such issue does not meet the private business tests of paragraphs (1) and (2) of section 141(b), and

children.label: iv
children.text: except as provided in subparagraph (D)(ii), repayments of principal on financing provided by the issue are used not later than the close of the 1st semiannual period beginning after the date the prepayment (or complete repayment) is received to redeem bonds which are part of such issue.

children.heading: Good faith effort to comply with mortgage eligibility requirements
children.label: B Good faith effort to comply with mortgage eligibility requirements
i
children.text: the issuer in good faith attempted to meet all such requirements before the mortgages were executed,

children.label: ii
children.text: 95 percent or more of the proceeds devoted to owner-financing was devoted to residences with respect to which (at the time the mortgages were executed) all such requirements were met, and

children.label: iii
children.text: any failure to meet the requirements of such subsections is corrected within a reasonable period after such failure is first discovered.

children.heading: Good faith effort to comply with other requirements
children.label: C Good faith effort to comply with other requirements
i
children.text: the issuer in good faith attempted to meet all such requirements, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any failure to meet such requirements is due to inadvertent error after taking reasonable steps to comply with such requirements.

children.heading: Proceeds must be used within 42 months of date of issuance
children.label: D Proceeds must be used within 42 months of date of issuance
I
children.text: all proceeds of the issue required to be used to finance owner-occupied residences are so used within the 42-month period beginning on the date of issuance of the issue (or, in the case of a refunding bond, within the 42-month period beginning on the date of issuance of the original bond) or, to the extent not so used within such period, are used within such period to redeem bonds which are part of such issue, and

children.label: II
children.text: no portion of the proceeds of the issue are used to make or finance any loan (other than a loan which is a nonpurpose investment within the meaning of section 148(f)(6)(A)) after the close of such period.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: Clause (i) (and clause (iv) of subparagraph (A)) shall not be construed to require amounts of less than $250,000 to be used to redeem bonds. The Secretary may by regulation treat related issues as 1 issue for purposes of the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Qualified veterans’ mortgage bond defined
children.label: b Qualified veterans’ mortgage bond defined

children.text: which is issued as part of an issue 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of which are to be used to provide residences for veterans,
the payment of the principal and interest on which is secured by the general obligation of a State,
which is part of an issue which meets the requirements of subsections (c), (g), (i)(1), and (), andl
which is part of an issue which does not meet the private business tests of paragraphs (1) and (2) of section 141(b).

children.label: A
children.text: it is a single-family residence which can reasonably be expected to become the principal residence of the mortgagor within a reasonable time after the financing is provided, and

children.label: B
children.text: it is located within the jurisdiction of the authority issuing the bond.

children.heading: For an issue
children.label: 2 For an issue
children.text: An issue meets the requirements of this subsection only if all of the residences for which owner-financing is provided under the issue meet the requirements of paragraph (1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An issue meets the requirements of this subsection only if 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of such issue are used to finance the residences of mortgagors who had no present ownership interest in their principal residences at any time during the 3-year period ending on the date their mortgage is executed.

children.label: A
children.text: financing with respect to targeted area residences,

children.label: B
children.text: qualified home improvement loans and qualified rehabilitation loans,

children.label: C
children.text: financing with respect to land described in subsection (i)(1)(C) and the construction of any residence thereon, and

children.label: D
children.text: in the case of bonds issued after the date of the enactment of this subparagraph, financing of any residence for a veteran (as defined in , United States Code), if such veteran has not previously qualified for and received such financing by reason of this subparagraph,section 101 of title 38

children.heading: Mortgagor’s interest in residence being financed
children.label: 3 Mortgagor’s interest in residence being financed
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), a mortgagor’s interest in the residence with respect to which the financing is being provided shall not be taken into account.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An issue meets the requirements of this subsection only if the acquisition cost of each residence the owner-financing of which is provided under the issue does not exceed 90 percent of the average area purchase price applicable to such residence.

children.heading: Average area purchase price
children.label: 2 Average area purchase price
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “average area purchase price” means, with respect to any residence, the average purchase price of single family residences (in the statistical area in which the residence is located) which were purchased during the most recent 12-month period for which sufficient statistical information is available. The determination under the preceding sentence shall be made as of the date on which the commitment to provide the financing is made (or, if earlier, the date of the purchase of the residence).

children.heading: Separate application to new residences and old residences
children.label: 3 Separate application to new residences and old residences
A
children.text: residences which have not been previously occupied, and

children.label: B
children.text: residences which have been previously occupied.

children.heading: Special rule for 2 to 4 family residences
children.label: 4 Special rule for 2 to 4 family residences
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, to the extent provided in regulations, the determination of average area purchase price shall be made separately with respect to 1 family, 2 family, 3 family, and 4 family residences.

children.heading: Special rule for targeted area residences
children.label: 5 Special rule for targeted area residences
children.text: In the case of a targeted area residence, paragraph (1) shall be applied by substituting “110 percent” for “90 percent”.

children.heading: Exception for qualified home improvement loans
children.label: 6 Exception for qualified home improvement loans
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply with respect to any qualified home improvement loan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An issue meets the requirements of this subsection only if all owner-financing provided under the issue is provided for mortgagors whose family income is 115 percent or less of the applicable median family income.

children.heading: Determination of family income
children.label: 2 Determination of family income
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the family income of mortgagors, and area median gross income, shall be determined by the Secretary after taking into account the regulations prescribed under section 8 of the United States Housing Act of 1937 (or, if such program is terminated, under such program as in effect immediately before such termination).

children.heading: Special rule for applying paragraph (1) in the case of targeted area residences
children.label: 3 Special rule for applying paragraph (1) in the case of targeted area residences
A
children.text: ⅓ of the amount of such financing may be provided without regard to paragraph (1), and

children.label: B
children.text: paragraph (1) shall be treated as satisfied with respect to the remainder of the owner financing if the family income of the mortgagor is 140 percent or less of the applicable median family income.

children.label: A
children.text: the area median gross income for the area in which such residence is located, or

children.label: B
children.text: the statewide median gross income for the State in which such residence is located.

children.heading: Adjustment of income requirement based on relation of high housing costs to income
children.label: 5 Adjustment of income requirement based on relation of high housing costs to income

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the residence (for which financing is provided under the issue) is located in a high housing cost area and the limitation determined under this paragraph is greater than the limitation otherwise applicable under paragraph (1), there shall be substituted for the income limitation in paragraph (1), a limitation equal to the percentage determined under subparagraph (B) of the area median gross income for such area.

children.heading: Income requirements for residences in high housing cost area
children.label: B Income requirements for residences in high housing cost area
I
children.text: 115 percent, and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount by which the housing cost/income ratio for such area exceeds 0.2.

children.heading: High housing cost areas
children.label: C High housing cost areas
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “high housing cost area” means any statistical area for which the housing cost/income ratio is greater than 1.2.

children.label: I
children.text: the applicable housing price ratio for such area, by

children.label: II
children.text: the ratio which the area median gross income for such area bears to the median gross income for the United States.

children.heading: Applicable housing price ratio
children.label: ii Applicable housing price ratio
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the applicable housing price ratio for any area is the new housing price ratio or the existing housing price ratio, whichever results in the housing cost/income ratio being closer to 1.

children.label: I
children.text: the average area purchase price (as defined in subsection (e)(2)) for residences described in subsection (e)(3)(A) which are located in such area bears to

children.label: II
children.text: the average purchase price (determined in accordance with the principles of subsection (e)(2)) for residences so described which are located in the United States.

children.heading: Existing housing price ratio
children.label: iv Existing housing price ratio
children.text: The existing housing price ratio for any area is the ratio determined in accordance with clause (iii) but with respect to residences described in subsection (e)(3)(B).

children.heading: Adjustment to income requirements based on family size
children.label: 6 Adjustment to income requirements based on family size
A
children.text: “100 percent” for “115 percent” each place it appears, and

children.label: B
children.text: “120 percent” for “140 percent” each place it appears.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An issue meets the requirements of this subsection only if such issue meets the requirements of paragraph (2) of this subsection and, in the case of an issue described in subsection (b)(1), such issue also meets the requirements of paragraph (3) of this subsection. Such requirements shall be in addition to the requirements of section 148.

children.heading: Effective rate of mortgage interest cannot exceed bond yield by more than 1.125 percentage points
children.label: 2 Effective rate of mortgage interest cannot exceed bond yield by more than 1.125 percentage points
i
children.text: the effective rate of interest on the mortgages provided under the issue, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the yield on the issue,

children.heading: Effective rate of mortgage interest
children.label: B Effective rate of mortgage interest

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In determining the effective rate of interest on any mortgage for purposes of this paragraph, there shall be taken into account all fees, charges, and other amounts borne by the mortgagor which are attributable to the mortgage or to the bond issue.

children.heading: Specification of some of the amounts to be treated as borne by the mortgagor
children.label: ii Specification of some of the amounts to be treated as borne by the mortgagor
I
children.text: all points or similar charges paid by the seller of the property, and

children.label: II
children.text: the excess of the amounts received from any person other than the mortgagor by any person in connection with the acquisition of the mortgagor’s interest in the property over the usual and reasonable acquisition costs of a person acquiring like property where owner-financing is not provided through the use of qualified mortgage bonds or qualified veterans’ mortgage bonds.

children.heading: Specification of some of the amounts to be treated as not borne by the mortgagor
children.label: iii Specification of some of the amounts to be treated as not borne by the mortgagor
I
children.text: any expected rebate of arbitrage profits, and

children.label: II
children.text: any application fee, survey fee, credit report fee, insurance charge, or similar amount to the extent such amount does not exceed amounts charged in such area in cases where owner-financing is not provided through the use of qualified mortgage bonds or qualified veterans’ mortgage bonds.

children.label: I
children.text: it shall be assumed that the mortgage prepayment rate will be the rate set forth in the most recent applicable mortgage maturity experience table published by the Federal Housing Administration, and

children.label: II
children.text: prepayments of principal shall be treated as received on the last day of the month in which the issuer reasonably expects to receive such prepayments.

children.label: i
children.text: the issue price (within the meaning of sections 1273 and 1274), and

children.label: ii
children.text: an expected maturity for the bonds which is consistent with the assumptions required under subparagraph (B)(iv).

children.heading: Arbitrage and investment gains to be used to reduce costs of owner-financing
children.label: 3 Arbitrage and investment gains to be used to reduce costs of owner-financing
I
children.text: the amount earned on all nonpurpose investments (other than investments attributable to an excess described in this clause), over

children.label: II
children.text: the amount which would have been earned if such investments were invested at a rate equal to the yield on the issue, plus

children.label: ii
children.text: any income attributable to the excess described in clause (i),

children.heading: Investment gains and losses
children.label: B Investment gains and losses
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), in determining the amount earned on all nonpurpose investments, any gain or loss on the disposition of such investments shall be taken into account.

children.heading: Reduction where issuer does not use full 1.125 percentage points under paragraph (2)
children.label: C Reduction where issuer does not use full 1.125 percentage points under paragraph (2)

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The amount required to be paid or credited to mortgagors under subparagraph (A) (determined under this paragraph without regard to this subparagraph) shall be reduced by the unused paragraph (2) amount.

children.heading: Unused paragraph (2) amount
children.label: ii Unused paragraph (2) amount
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the unused paragraph (2) amount is the amount which (if it were treated as an interest payment made by mortgagors) would result in the excess referred to in paragraph (2)(A) being equal to 1.125 percentage points. Such amount shall be fixed and determined as of the yield determination date.

children.heading: Election to pay United States
children.label: D Election to pay United States
i
children.text: not less frequently than once each 5 years after the date of issue, an amount equal to 90 percent of the aggregate amount which would be required to be paid or credited to mortgagors under subparagraph (A) (and not theretofore paid to the United States), and

children.label: ii
children.text: not later than 60 days after the redemption of the last bond, 100 percent of such aggregate amount not theretofore paid to the United States.

children.heading: Simplified accounting
children.label: E Simplified accounting
children.text: The Secretary shall permit any simplified system of accounting for purposes of this paragraph which the issuer establishes to the satisfaction of the Secretary will assure that the purposes of this paragraph are carried out.

children.heading: Nonpurpose investment
children.label: F Nonpurpose investment
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “nonpurpose investment” has the meaning given such term by section 148(f)(6)(A).

children.heading: Portion of loans required to be placed in targeted areas
children.label: h Portion of loans required to be placed in targeted areas

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An issue meets the requirements of this subsection only if at least 20 percent of the proceeds of the issue which are devoted to providing owner-financing is made available (with reasonable diligence) for owner-financing of targeted area residences for at least 1 year after the date on which owner-financing is first made available with respect to targeted area residences.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: Nothing in paragraph (1) shall be treated as requiring the making available of an amount which exceeds 40 percent of the average annual aggregate principal amount of mortgages executed during the immediately preceding 3 calendar years for single-family, owner-occupied residences located in targeted areas within the jurisdiction of the issuing authority.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An issue meets the requirements of this subsection only if no part of the proceeds of such issue is used to acquire or replace existing mortgages.

children.label: i
children.text: construction period loans,

children.label: ii
children.text: bridge loans or similar temporary initial financing, and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a qualified rehabilitation, an existing mortgage,

children.label: C Exception for certain contract for deed agreements

children.text: the seller’s remedy for nonpayment is forfeiture rather than judicial or nonjudicial foreclosure.

children.label: 2 Certain requirements must be met where mortgage is assumed
children.text: An issue meets the requirements of this subsection only if each mortgage with respect to which owner-financing has been provided under such issue may be assumed only if the requirements of subsections (c), (d), and (e), and the requirements of paragraph (1) or (3)(B) of subsection (f) (whichever applies), are met with respect to such assumption.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “qualified census tract” means a census tract in which 70 percent or more of the families have income which is 80 percent or less of the statewide median family income.

children.heading: Data used
children.label: B Data used
children.text: The determination under subparagraph (A) shall be made on the basis of the most recent decennial census for which data are available.

children.heading: Criteria to be used in approving State designations
children.label: B Criteria to be used in approving State designations
i
children.text: the condition of the housing stock, including the age of the housing and the number of abandoned and substandard residential units,

children.label: ii
children.text: the need of area residents for owner-financing under this section, as indicated by low per capita income, a high percentage of families in poverty, a high number of welfare recipients, and high unemployment rates,

children.label: iii
children.text: the potential for use of owner-financing under this section to improve housing conditions in the area, and

children.label: iv
children.text: the existence of a housing assistance plan which provides a displacement program and a public improvements and services program.

children.label: k Other definitions and special rules

children.heading: Metropolitan statistical area
children.label: B Metropolitan statistical area
children.text: The term “metropolitan statistical area” includes the area defined as such by the Secretary of Commerce.

children.heading: Designation where adequate statistical information not available
children.label: C Designation where adequate statistical information not available
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, if there is insufficient recent statistical information with respect to a county (or portion thereof) described in subparagraph (A)(ii), the Secretary may substitute for such county (or portion thereof) another area for which there is sufficient recent statistical information.

children.heading: Designation where no county
children.label: D Designation where no county
children.text: In the case of any portion of a State which is not within a county, subparagraphs (A)(ii) and (C) shall be applied by substituting for “county” an area designated by the Secretary which is the equivalent of a county.

children.label: C Special rule for qualified rehabilitation loans
children.text: In the case of a qualified rehabilitation loan, for purposes of subsection (e), the term “acquisition cost” includes the cost of the rehabilitation.

children.text: 75 percent or more of the existing internal structural framework of such building is retained in place, and

children.text: the expenditures for such rehabilitation are 25 percent or more of the mortgagor’s adjusted basis in the residence.

children.heading: Determinations on actuarial basis
children.label: 6 Determinations on actuarial basis
children.text: All determinations of yield, effective interest rates, and amounts required to be paid or credited to mortgagors or paid to the United States under subsection (g) shall be made on an actuarial basis taking into account the present value of money.

children.text: any indebtedness of the corporation allocable to the dwelling unit shall be treated as if it were indebtedness of the shareholder entitled to occupy the dwelling unit.

children.heading: Adjustment to targeted area requirement
children.label: B Adjustment to targeted area requirement
children.text: In the case of any issue to provide financing to a cooperative housing corporation with respect to cooperative housing not located in a targeted area, to the extent provided in regulations, such issue may be combined with 1 or more other issues for purposes of determining whether the requirements of subsection (h) are met.

children.heading: Cooperative housing corporation
children.label: C Cooperative housing corporation
children.text: The term “cooperative housing corporation” has the meaning given to such term by section 216(b)(1).

i

children.heading: Bonds subject to qualified mortgage bond termination date
children.label: B Bonds subject to qualified mortgage bond termination date
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any bond issued after the date specified in subsection (a)(1)(B).

children.heading: Limited equity cooperative housing
children.label: C Limited equity cooperative housing
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “limited equity cooperative housing” means any dwelling unit which a person is entitled to occupy by reason of his ownership of stock in a qualified cooperative housing corporation.

children.label: ii

children.heading: Effect of election
children.label: E Effect of election
children.text: If a cooperative housing corporation makes an election under this paragraph, section 216 shall not apply with respect to such corporation (or any successor thereof) during the qualified project period (as defined in section 142(d)(2)).

children.heading: Corporation must continue to be qualified cooperative
children.label: F Corporation must continue to be qualified cooperative
children.text: Subparagraph (A)(i) shall not apply to limited equity cooperative housing unless the cooperative housing corporation continues to be a qualified cooperative housing corporation at all times during the qualified project period (as defined in section 142(d)(2)).

children.heading: Election irrevocable
children.label: G Election irrevocable
children.text: Any election under this paragraph, once made, shall be irrevocable.

children.label: 10 Treatment of resale price control and subsidy lien programs

children.text: In the case of a residence which is located in a high housing cost area (as defined in section 143(f)(5)), the interest of a governmental unit in such residence by reason of financing provided under any qualified program shall not be taken into account under this section (other than subsection (m)), and the acquisition cost of the residence which is taken into account under subsection (e) shall be such cost reduced by the amount of such financing.

children.label: l Additional requirements for qualified veterans’ mortgage bonds

children.label: 1 Veterans to whom financing may be provided
children.text: An issue meets the requirements of this paragraph only if each mortgagor to whom financing is provided under the issue is a qualified veteran.

children.heading: Special rules relating to limitation on recapture amount based on gain realized
children.label: 6 Special rules relating to limitation on recapture amount based on gain realized

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), gain shall be taken into account whether or not recognized, and the adjusted basis of the taxpayer’s interest in the residence shall be determined without regard to sections 1033(b) and 1034(e) (as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Taxpayer Relief Act of 1997) for purposes of determining gain.

children.heading: Dispositions other than sales, exchanges, and involuntary conversions
children.label: B Dispositions other than sales, exchanges, and involuntary conversions
children.text: In the case of a disposition other than a sale, exchange, or involuntary conversion, gain shall be determined as if the interest had been sold for its fair market value.

children.heading: Involuntary conversions resulting from casualties
children.label: C Involuntary conversions resulting from casualties
children.text: In the case of property which (as a result of its destruction in whole or in part by fire, storm, or other casualty) is compulsorily or involuntarily converted, paragraph (1) shall not apply to such conversion if the taxpayer purchases (during the period specified in section 1033(a)(2)(B)) property for use as his principal residence on the site of the converted property. For purposes of subparagraph (A), the adjusted basis of the taxpayer in the residence shall not be adjusted for any gain or loss on a conversion to which this subparagraph applies.

children.heading: No basis adjustment
children.label: A No basis adjustment
children.text: No adjustment shall be made to the basis of any property for the increase in tax under this subsection.

children.heading: Special rule where 2 or more persons hold interests in residence
children.label: B Special rule where 2 or more persons hold interests in residence
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C) and in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, if 2 or more persons hold interests in any residence and are jointly liable for the federally-subsidized indebtedness, the recapture amount shall be determined separately with respect to their respective interests in the residence.

children.heading: Transfers to spouses and former spouses
children.label: C Transfers to spouses and former spouses
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any transfer on which no gain or loss is recognized under section 1041. In any such case, the transferee shall be treated under this subsection in the same manner as the transferor would have been treated had such transfer not occurred.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: D Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out this subsection, including regulations dealing with dispositions of partial interests in a residence.
